winner: A 3D printer in New York just manufactured a gun so precise, it fired a bullet that was *also* 3D printed—turns out, it had a tiny QR code on its base for the serial number. The FBI is now asking, "Why didn't anyone think to 3D print a *higher caliber* first?

candidates.text: A 3D printer in New York just manufactured a gun so precise, it fired a bullet that was *also* 3D printed—turns out, it had a tiny QR code on its base for the serial number. The FBI is now asking, "Why didn't anyone think to 3D print a *higher caliber* first?

candidates.text: Why did the 3D-printed gun fail its first date? It turned out to be a real *plastic* disappointment.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "Puns on 'plastic' and 'disappointment' are clever and specific to the context of 3D-printed guns, but the joke is slightly generic and lacks deeper surprise."}

candidates.text: Why did the 3D-printed gun go to therapy? It had too many layers to unpack!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses wordplay on 'layers' (both literal and metaphorical), and ties back to the headline. However, it's somewhat predictable and lacks deeper specificity or surprise."}

candidates.text: A guy walks into a store and buys a 3D-printed gun. The clerk says, "That'll be $20." He replies, "Really? I thought those were free now.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific to the topic, but the humor is weak and relies on a tired 'free now' punchline that doesn't fully connect with the headline or context."}

candidates.text: Why did the gun decide to go for a 3D makeover? It wanted to make sure everyone saw its *firing* potential!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'firing potential,' but it's a bit forced and lacks surprise. The reference to 3D-printed guns in NYC isn't fully leveraged for specificity or humor."}
